topPRs.title: feat(app): harden desktop shell and view apps
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

- Harden desktop/web shell view switching, app-control view commands, plugin lifecycle/view registry behavior, and cloud/dev launch paths.
- Add simple Notes/Calendar test views plus calendar LifeOps HTTP routes so view switching and view-backed actions have concrete plugin surfaces to exercise.
- Polish launcher UX: home <-> springboard and inner springboard pages now follow the pointer during swipe, then settle with a spring-like transition; springboard tiles render clean deterministic app icons instead of cropped preview/hero images.
- Expand regression coverage for continuous chat gestures, shell layout stability, springboard interaction, view switching, app-control, calendar routes, plugin loading, and dev smoke helpers.

## Why

This branch packages the local desktop/cloud shell QA work into one draft PR so maintainers can review it against current `develop`. The main user-facing issues addressed are laggy/snap-only launcher paging, messy app icons, fragile chat/shell gesture states, and missing concrete test plugin views for notes/calendar style workflows.

## Impact

- UI users should see smoother launcher paging and cleaner app icons across browser, desktop, and mobile webviews.
- App-control can target and reason about view context more reliably.
- Calendar/simple view plugin surfaces give QA and agents real view-backed actions instead of fake-only paths.
- Calendar unit config now aliases source workspace packages consistently and keeps real-db tests out of the unit lane.

## Checks

- `bunx @biomejs/biome check packages/ui/src/components/shell/HomeSpringboardSurface.tsx packages/ui/src/components/shell/HomeSpringboardSurface.composed.test.tsx packages/ui/src/components/views/ViewTileImage.tsx packages/ui/src/components/pages/Springboard.tsx packages/ui/src/components/pages/Springboard.test.tsx plugins/plugin-calendar/vitest.config.ts`

topPRs.title: feat(plugin-embeddings): provider-agnostic standalone TEXT_EMBEDDING plugin
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
Closes #9567.

## What
A new standalone **`@elizaos/plugin-embeddings`** — a provider-agnostic ("bring your own") `TEXT_EMBEDDING` provider. Point one set of `EMBEDDING_*` vars at **any** OpenAI-compatible `/embeddings` endpoint (personal OpenAI key, Eliza Cloud, Voyage, Together, local TEI/Infinity/vLLM/LM Studio) and embeddings work **independent of the chat brain**.

## Why
Embeddings were coupled to the chat provider. A self-hosted bot on **Claude** (no embeddings API) or **Cerebras** (no `/embeddings`) had no clean path to real embeddings — `plugin-elizacloud` bundles `TEXT_EMBEDDING` with priority-50 text handlers that hijack the chat brain and is gated to a cloud `eliza_xxx` key; `plugin-openai`'s `OPENAI_EMBEDDING_*` override only works when plugin-openai is itself enabled and smuggles non-OpenAI providers under an `OPENAI_` namespace. This generalizes the existing `plugin-lmstudio` (`capabilities:["embedding"]`) pattern into a chat-agnostic one.

## Design (purely additive, zero regression)
- Registers **only** `TEXT_EMBEDDING` + `TEXT_EMBEDDING_BATCH` — no text/image/audio handlers, no actions/providers/services.
- **Load-gated**: auto-enables only when `EMBEDDING_BASE_URL` or `EMBEDDING_API_KEY` is set → not loaded on any existing deployment.
- **Priority `1`**: `local-inference @0 < plugin-embeddings @1 < Eliza Cloud @50` — opt-in beats a bare local embedder, yields to a paired Cloud. (This is the one product call from #9567 — happy to change the band.)
- **Throws on every real failure** (issue #9324 / Commandment 8) — never a zero/garbage vector. Null boot-probe returns a correctly-sized marker for the dimension probe only.
- Raw `fetch` (no `@ai-sdk` dep); `@elizaos/core` peer only. Browser build suppresses the `Authorization` header unless `EMBEDDING_BROWSER_URL` (server proxy) is set.
- **Zero changes** to core / plugin-sql / plugin-openai / plugin-elizacloud / plugin-local-inference.

### Mode matrix
| Mode (no `EMBEDDING_*`) | embeddings | Δ |
|---|---|---|
| cloud | plugin-elizacloud @50 | **identical** |
| local | plugin-local-inference @0 | **identical** |
| self-hosted Claude/Cerebras (opt in) | this plugin → any endpoint | **new capability** |

## Evidence (real, live)
Live-tested on a self-hosted **Claude-chat** bot (`provider=anthropic` throughout), same plugin, two providers by swapping one var:
- **OpenAI Direct** (`EMBEDDING_BASE_URL=https://api.openai.com/v1`): `[Embeddings] POST https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings model=text-embedding-3-small` ×2 (store + recall), stored a memory, Claude chat unaffected.
- **Eliza Cloud** (`EMBEDDING_BASE_URL=https://www.elizacloud.ai/api/v1`): boot init resolved the cloud endpoint; a recall query returned **"Teal."** — correctly recalling the memory stored under OpenAI embeddings (same model/dim → compatible vectors). Zero embedding errors.
- 27/27 unit tests (null-probe width, wire-mocked vector, dimension-mismatch/empty/unsupported/no-endpoint/non-OK throws, batch ordering, `VECTOR_DIMS` contract), build + lint clean.

Screenshots/video N/A (server-side embedding plumbing; backend logs above are the evidence). Audio N/A.

## Follow-up (not this PR)
openai/openrouter/elizacloud ~95% duplicate a hand-rolled `fetch(${baseURL}/embeddings)` — a shared OpenAI-compatible embeddings client is a worthwhile separate cleanup.

topPRs.title: feat(#9565): boot startup telemetry, first-paint reduction, launch orange #ef5a1f
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Closes part of #9565 (boot/startup optimization epic).

## What

Optimizes the device agent boot/startup readiness path end to end, building on
the renderer-surface orange already landed by #9587 (which fixed only
`index.html` + `StartupShell`, left native launch configs at `#FF5800`, and set
`StartupShell` to `var(--bg,…)` which renders the theme white/black — see proof).

### 1. Measure — renderer startup trace + unified trace id
`@elizaos/ui/state/startup-telemetry` — a dependency-free recorder emitting
`performance.mark()`s at the documented cold-start checkpoints and mirroring them
to `window.__ELIZA_STARTUP_TRACE__`. It only **observes**; `useStartupCoordinator`
stays the sole startup authority and the pure reducer is untouched. The trace id
adopts a native-host-injected `window.__ELIZA_STARTUP_TRACE_ID__` when present
(one id across native host ↔ renderer ↔ backend boot), else derives a
renderer-local id + records `timeOrigin` for time-based correlation.

Checkpoints: `module-eval`, `main-start`, `app-modules:start/end`,
`bridges:start/end`, `react-mount:start/end`, `startup-shell:first-paint`,
`coordinator:<phase>` (incl. `coordinator:ready`).

### 2. Optimize — shrink the first-paint critical path
`initializeAppModules()` blocked the first React mount on **10** plugin imports;
the boot config reads only **3** synchronously. The other 7 (personal-assistant,
vincent, task-coordinator ×2, phone, steward, training) are imported for
side-effect registration / `React.lazy` pre-warm only → moved to the existing
idle path (`BOOT_CONFIG_DEFERRED_MODULE_LOADERS`). Measured `app-modules` span:
**~33–44 ms warm** (was a blocking await of 10 imports).

### 3. Launch orange = `#ef5a1f` (no flash)
Every boot/loading/launch surface now uses `#ef5a1f` (= `DEFAULT_BACKGROUND_COLOR`,
the home `ShaderBackground`) so native splash → React `StartupShell` → home is a
single seamless orange. `StartupShell` uses a dedicated `--launch-bg` token
(**proven** to render `rgb(239,90,31)` = `#ef5a1f`; `--bg` resolves to the theme
`rgb(245,245,244)` near-white, so develop's `var(--bg,…)` would flash near-white).
Native surfaces fixed: `capacitor.config.ts`, `app.config.ts` web colors + PWA
manifest, Android `colors.xml`/`styles.xml`, iOS `LaunchScreen.storyboard`. Brand
accent stays `#FF5800`.

## Guardrails
- `packages/app/test/first-paint-guard.test.ts` — fails CI on a new blocking
  plugin import in `initializeAppModules()`.
- `packages/app/test/brand-surface.test.ts` — every launch surface = `#ef5a1f`
  (tied to `DEFAULT_BACKGROUND_COLOR`); brand stays `#FF5800`.
- `packages/ui/src/state/startup-telemetry.test.ts` — recording/dedup/host-id/
  window mirror.

## Docs + harness
- `packages/app/docs/device-boot-startup.md` — per-platform boot sequence
  diagrams (desktop/Android/iOS/web-remote), checkpoint catalog, cross-process
  trace-id seam, baseline matrix status.
- `scripts/capture-startup-trace.mjs` (`bun run --cwd packages/app trace:startup`)
  — repeatable Playwright trace capture (cold/warm).

## Evidence (`.github/issue-evidence/9565-startup-readiness/`)
- `desktop-web-renderer-trace-m4max.json` + `renderer-trace-second-capture.json`
  — real cold/warm renderer traces (app-modules span 33–44 ms).
- `startup-shell-orange-ef5a1f.png` + `startup-orange-proof.md` — computed
  `backgroundColor: rgb(239,90,31)` = `#ef5a1f`.

## Tests
`packages/app`: brand-surface (9) + first-paint-guard (3) green.
`packages/ui`: startup-telemetry (8) + startup-coordinator/useStartupCoordinator/
startup-shell (20) green. Lint + typecheck clean for touched files.

## Non-goals honored
No second boot state machine (extends the coordinator + observes). Agent is not
marked ready before first-turn deps. No LifeOps/health or media-store changes.

topPRs.title: feat(voice): wire live AEC echoReference + on-device delay calibration (#9583)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Follow-up to #9455 / #9575. The NLMS echo canceller seam in `audio-frame-consumer.ts` was ready, but on the live device path nothing supplied the far-end reference and the playback→mic delay was never calibrated. This wires both.

## What this delivers (the three #9583 checkboxes)

- [x] **Wire the `echoReference` provider on the live device path.** New `PlaybackReferenceBuffer` (a bounded, timestamped ring of the agent's TTS playback PCM) + `createEchoReferenceProvider` factory, owned by `LiveDiarizationSession` and passed to the `AudioFrameConsumer`. The device feeds the far-end via a new `POST /api/voice/audio-frames/playback` route. When no playback covers a mic window the provider returns `null` and the canceller is skipped entirely (zero idle cost, exact passthrough).
- [x] **On-device playback→mic delay calibration per platform.** `echo-delay-calibrator.ts` estimates the bulk delay by normalized cross-correlation (GCC-style) over an echo-only window, with a per-platform seed table (`darwin`/`ios`/`android`/`win32`/`linux`). The session seeds from `process.platform` and auto-refines on the first echo-only burst; the active delay + confidence are surfaced in `/api/voice/audio-frames/status`.
- [x] **(Optional) AEC3-class residual-echo suppression.** Opt-in single-band residual suppressor on the canceller output, gated strictly to echo-only frames (far-end active, no double-talk) so it can never suppress a barge-in. Default off; enabled per device via `ELIZA_VOICE_AEC_RESIDUAL_SUPPRESSION`.

## Evidence

40 vitest tests, all green, typecheck clean:
- `playback-reference-buffer.test.ts` — write/read alignment, gaps→null, eviction, delay offset, **end-to-end** proof that buffer→provider→consumer→canceller cancels the agent echo by **>10 dB ERLE**.
- `echo-delay-calibrator.test.ts` — recovers a known delay (±2 samples, confidence >0.7), zero-delay, silence→0, and does **not** false-lock on uncorrelated near-end speech.
- `nlms-echo-canceller.test.ts` — residual suppressor is a no-op by default, further attenuates echo-only residual when on, never attenuates the user while the agent is silent, and does not crush the user during double-talk.

```
Test Files  4 passed (4)
     Tests  40 passed (40)
```

The actual playback-capture wiring (device-side) and per-device delay tuning still happen on hardware; this lands the reusable agent-side machinery + seams + auto-calibration so a device run just supplies frames.

topPRs.title: De-larp the benchmark suite: delete unwired benchmarks, wire PARTIAL→REAL, scheduled orchestrator lane + CI-coverage gate (#9475)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## De-larp the benchmark suite (#9475)

Verified every claim against the code before acting (this is a "verify every benchmark actually benchmarks Eliza" task), then implemented the full checklist. **6 commits**, each test-verified.

### ✅ Delete (LARP / shim / dup — confirmed zero `@elizaos` wiring + zero registry entry)
Deleted the dirs + **fully unwired them from the orchestrator** (they were entangled in `code_agent_matrix.py`, `adapters.py`, `code_agent_coverage.py`, `random_baseline_runner.py`, `runner.py`, `matrix_validation.py`, and ~20 tests — not a simple `git rm`):
- vendored upstreams: `claw-eval`, `qwen-claw-bench`, `swe-bench-pro`, `swe-bench-multilingual`, `loca-bench`
- `qwen-web-bench` (stub), `compactbench` (judges compactors, not the agent), `evm` (abandoned)
- import-shim / dup matrix adapters: `app_eval`, `clawbench_matrix`, `claw_eval_matrix`, `qwen_claw_bench_matrix`, `swe_bench_pro_matrix`, `openclaw_benchmark`
- `skillsbench` / `elizaos_mmau` / `mmau` were already gone on `develop`.
- Also cleaned deletion **stragglers** in KEPT code: removed the orphaned `eliza-adapter` EVM bridge + test, and fixed `app-eval/test_code_agent_coding.py` + docs that imported the removed `benchmarks.app_eval` shim.

### ✅ Wire up (PARTIAL → REAL)
- **rlm-bench**: registry + CLI `--mode` default `stub` → `rlm` (real inference).
- **adhdbench**: `--provider` default → `eliza` (real bridge); `mock-passthrough` now opt-in.
- **experience**: `--mode` default `direct` (no LLM) → `eliza-bridge` (real AgentRuntime).
- **interrupt-bench**: default `scripted` → `cerebras` (real model).
  - *Finding:* the orchestrator adapter layer already routed adhd/experience/interrupt/rlm through the real bridge by default — the larp was only the bare CLI/registry default, now fixed (belt-and-suspenders).
- **three-agent-dialogue**: genuinely IGNORED → **registered** as an orchestrator adapter (command + scorer reading `verification.json` + synthetic calibration payload; satisfies the discovery/calibration/cross-matrix invariants).
- **personality-bench**: already adapter-registered with real runtime (confirmed).
- **app-eval**: its deterministic keyword scorer is **intentional/reproducible** (AGENTS.md). Added an **opt-in** LLM judge (`APP_EVAL_LLM_JUDGE=1`) that *augments* it (default unchanged) — pure, unit-tested blending.

### ✅ Relabel (REAL but not measuring the Eliza agent)
Added honest top-of-README notes to `standard/` (raw-model academic), `scambench` + `abliteration-robustness` (generic provider/model eval), `loadperf/` + `mobile-resource/` (infra/device KPIs).

### ✅ Fix CI + docs (highest value)
- **Scheduled orchestrator lane**: `.github/workflows/benchmark-orchestrator-scheduled.yml` — weekly (+dispatch) real-model run of the core subset (`bfcl,action-calling,agentbench,tau_bench,mint,context_bench`) via `python -m benchmarks.orchestrator run --provider groq`, gated on `GROQ_API_KEY` (skips gracefully), with subset-scoped publishability + artifact upload.
- **Smoke/manual marker + test**: `orchestrator/ci_coverage.py` classifies every registered benchmark into a CI lane (`scheduled`/`smoke`/`manual`); `tests/test_ci_coverage.py` keeps it **1:1 with the registry** so no benchmark silently gets zero coverage.
- **README rewrite**: replaced the "40+" framing with the honest breakdown (43 registered / ~25 bridge-wired-real / explicit CI lanes), documents what was deleted and the new lanes.

### Validation
- `pytest orchestrator/tests + tests` → **480 passed, 1 failed**. The one failure (`test_hermes_env_all_incomplete_zero_is_not_publishable`) is **pre-existing on `develop`** (hermes-env scoring, untouched by this PR, unrelated to #9475).
- `orchestrator list-benchmarks` → 51 adapters, **all benchmark dirs covered**.
- app-eval judge tests (9) + code-agent-coding tests green.
- New workflow YAML validated; comprehensive grep confirms **zero remaining references** to any deleted dir/module.

Closes #9475
